title: TMC lurches from crisis to crisis: Mamata's party battles for survival as rebel MPs move to stake claim
content: TMC, which had been running its Delhi office from Partha Bhowmick’s bungalow at 20, Dr Rajendra Prasad Road since last year, moved to 61 South Avenue about a week ago after it learnt that Bhowmick was among the dissidents, according to party sources. It had earlier been operating out of Nadimul Haque’s residence, to which it has now returned.

title: Demographic panel to visit metros, industrial & border areas to study population changes
content: Home minister Amit Shah Saturday chaired a meeting with senior MHA officials to review arrangements for proper facilitation of the committee. The first meeting of the panel has already been convened and its agenda formulated. “MHA will provide logistical and other necessary support for proper functioning of the committee,” a ministry official said.

title: Will voice aspirations of Global South at G7 summit: PM Modi
content: After his meeting with Macron on Sunday in Nice, Modi will travel to Slovakia for the first ever visit by an Indian PM to the country since its independence in 1993 before returning to France for the Evian G7 summit on June 16-17. Modi is expected to underscore India’s concerns over the consequences of the West Asia conflict, including the energy crisis, for the Global South in his engagements while stressing the significance of freedom of navigation through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Shubman Gill enters elite club, becomes fastest Indian to...
content: Shubman Gill etched his name in history, becoming the fastest Indian to 3,000 ODI runs in just 61 innings against Afghanistan. This remarkable feat places him second globally, behind Hashim Amla. Gill's consistent brilliance and attacking flair solidify his status as a premier world batter, adding another jewel to his young, glittering career.

title: Canada's sixth-largest state Manitoba retires student pathway to permanent residency
content: Manitoba has closed its Career Employment Pathway for international graduates, effective immediately. Candidates with active EOIs and six months of provincial work experience can now be considered under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba Pathway. This change aims to align graduates' skills with labor market needs, prioritizing those who studied and are working in the province.

title: 5 Indian Air Force men killed in An-32 crash, co-pilot survives
content: IAF extends its deepest condolences to the bereaved families and stands firmly with them in this hour of grief,” IAF said in a post on X on Saturday. Giving details, IAF said the “aircraft met with an accident during a routine sortie today at approximately 1000h at Jorhat, Assam. Crash site management and initial enquiries are on at this time.”

title: Seafarers are rarely aware of sanctioned, ‘dark ship’ status
content: Captain Savio Ramos, general secretary of Maritime Union of India (MUI) said a sailor has the option to seek de-boarding from a ship once it enters the restricted zone and as per norms, the company concerned is bound to send them back to town and most firms have done so and changed crew. He added that sailors concerned need to decide and take a call.

title: CPM asks Congress to stop being ‘facilitator for ED, Modi govt’
content: Ahead of the INDIA bloc meeting, Baby in a sharply worded letter to Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge had pointed to allegations by Rahul Gandhi and other senior Congress leaders during the Kerala polls of CPI(M) and the BJP colluding and calls for ED action against then CM Pinarayi Vijayan. The Left party had asked the Congress to “clear the air regarding such disruptive moves” with CPI(M)’s Rajya Sabha MP John Brittas strongly raising the matter at the INDIA meeting.

title: US ban on Anthropic models sparks AI sovereignty concerns
content: The two use the same underlying model and are distinguished only by their safeguards: Fable 5 is the safeguarded version released to the public, while Mythos 5, with those cyber restrictions lifted, had been reserved for a small group of vetted partners. Cybersecurity experts warn that broad restrictions could undermine defensive security efforts while accelerating calls for sovereign AI capabilities.

title: India remains number 2 Russian oil buyer in May
content: CREA said India imported Russian hydrocarbons worth a total of 5.8 billion euros ($6.7 billion) and crude oil constituted 83% of the purchase. China remained biggest importer of Russian energy with purchases totalling nearly 7 billion euros ($8.1 billion), the report said.

title: Brazil vs Morocco live score: FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C updates
content: Brazil and Morocco are set for one of the standout matches of the FIFA World Cup 2026 group stage. Brazil begin their search for a sixth world title under Carlo Ancelotti, while Morocco look to continue the momentum created by their historic 2022 campaign. With stars such as Vinicius Junior, Achraf Hakimi and Brahim Diaz on the field, fans can expect a competitive and high-quality contest from the opening whistle.

title: India joins elite group of nations with BMD capability as DRDO conducts 3 missile tests
content: Separately, a maiden flight-test of the Naval Anti-Ship Missile-Medium Range (NASM-MR) was carried out successfully. The missile demonstrated pinpoint navigation, low-level sea-skimming flight and terminal accuracy against maritime targets. Congratulating DRDO, defence minister Rajnath Singh, in a post on X on Saturday, said, “Multi-layered BMD capability was successfully demonstrated. These tests demonstrate multiple crucial technologies to bolster the country’s defence capabilities against different types of enemy threats.

title: Bill Gates isn't happy with US govt taking stake in Intel, IBM & other US companies
content: Bill Gates has raised concerns over the Trump administration taking equity stakes in Intel, IBM and other US companies, saying that the "rules of the game are pretty unclear right now." The Microsoft co-founder warned Washington could favour firms it owns over rivals with better technology. His comments come as Trump plans to meet AI executives from OpenAI, Anthropic and others to discuss similar government stakes.

title: Indian man sentenced to 34 years in UK for kidnap, torture and rape
content: The court heard how the victim – a 24-year-old woman – became suspicious when she was asked to deliver the suitcase from Thailand back to the UK in June 2024. She refused, and when she arrived at Birmingham Airport she was put into a car and driven to London by masked men. She was taken to a house in Hanwell, west London, where she was subjected to prolonged torture, including by Singh, who also raped her on two occasions, the court was told.

title: Need for ‘antifragile system’: PM Modi's principal secretary PK Mishra
content: Mishra added while traditional approaches often focus on ‘bouncing back’ after disruption, the emerging paradigm requires systems that can learn, adapt, and emerge stronger through disruption, moving towards bouncing forward and increasingly becoming antifragile systems. “The objective today is not only recovery but building stronger institutions, infrastructure and governance systems capable of managing future shocks and uncertainties,” he added.

title: After Mumbai birthday cake ordeal, pins & wires in food banned
content: The nationwide advisory, issued by Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) on June 12, follows reports of metallic pins and wires being found in cakes and attached to food packets. “There exists a significant risk that such pins may inadvertently be consumed by consumers, resulting in injury and adverse health consequences,” FSSAI said in its advisory.

title: Ronaldinho extends heartfelt support for Cristiano Ronaldo’s Portugal in a statement
content: Brazil and Portugal enter the 2026 FIFA World Cup from very different positions, yet a single comment from Ronaldinho has linked the two football powers. The Brazilian legend made it clear that Brazil remains his first choice, but if the Seleção fall short, he would like to see Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal finally lift football's biggest prize.

title: Jordan Kyrou and New York Islanders trade rumors gain momentum ahead of NHL offseason
content: The New York Islanders are once again linked to Jordan Kyrou via NHL trade rumours as the offseason approaches. The St. Louis Blues forward had previously been linked to New York before talks fell through at the trade deadline. The Islanders need more offence, and the Blues are trying to figure out what they want to do long term, reigniting speculation about Kyrou’s future.

title: James Harden arrested in Houston for carrying unlawful weapon in car
content: NBA star James Harden was arrested in Houston early Saturday morning. Police reportedly found a handgun in his vehicle during a traffic stop. Harden was detained and later released on bond. The charge is a misdemeanor for unlawful carrying of a weapon. Harden recently joined the Cleveland Cavaliers. He is scheduled to appear in court on June 22.

title: Centre asks Telangana CM to probe 1,600 crore missing coal
content: In his letter, the coal minister said the allegations could impact the financial stability of SCCL, especially in the backdrop of its current financial position due to unpaid dues exceeding Rs 51,500 crore by Telangana govt. SCCL is jointly owned by the Telangana govt and the Centre in a 51:49 equity ratio and has a workforce of nearly 40,000 employees.

title: EAM Jaishankar calls Marco Rubio on sailor deaths, US stands by attacks
content: After having twice summoned the American envoy over US military strikes on foreign-flagged ships carrying Indian mariners, India further escalated the issue as external affairs minister S Jaishankar reiterated India’s protest in a phone call with his US counterpart Marco Rubio. “Such lethal actions against commercial shipping are not justified,” Jaishankar later posted on X.

title: 7 Indians arrested in illegal working crackdown at parcel delivery depots in UK
content: Seven Indian nationals, one Ghanaian national and one Pakistani national were identified as immigration offenders. One Indian national was arrested at DPD Newbury and six Indian nationals were arrested at DPD Basingstoke. All have been placed on immigration bail, subject to strict conditions, while further inquiries take place. If they are found to have been working unlawfully, they will be removed from the UK.

title: First batch of 5 women NDA entrants commissioned into Indian Air Force
content: Defence minister Rajnath Singh, who reviewed the Combined Graduation Parade of 217 Course and awarded 'President's Commission' to the graduating cadets, advised them to adapt when necessary, in war-like situations, and to innovate while executing to gain an edge. Extending special congratulations to women officers, the minister said the growing presence of Nari Shakti will further strengthen IAF, making it more robust and balanced.

title: How rich is a trillionaire? Elon Musk can sponsor IPL for 17,000 years, spend Rs 1 crore daily for 23,500 years
content: Elon Musk's ascent to trillionaire status, driven by Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI, redefines immense wealth. A trillion dollars, approximately Rs 95 lakh crore, dwarfs national economies and government budgets. This fortune could fund multiple Apollo missions, build numerous advanced telescopes, or purchase over 75 aircraft carriers, illustrating wealth on a scale previously unimaginable.

title: After SC's NDA verdict, Flt Cdt Minakshi among first women to join IAF
content: In mid-2021, the Supreme Court's historic verdict shattered a glass ceiling with NDA opening its gates to women. Minakshi seized the moment and cleared the examination, conquered SSB, and after nine months of waiting, received the call letter. On 6th Aug 2022, she stepped into NDA, making history.  On May 30, 2025, dressed in gleaming white patrols, she crossed the hallowed 'Antim Pag' at Khetarpal Parade Ground, earning the prestigious title —ex-NDA.

title: Explained: Why fuel, fertilisers & forex are so important right now
content: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman recently urged the country to focus on fuel, fertiliser and foreign exchange and underlined that PM Narendra Modi’s appeal to conserve foreign exchange was "very important" amid the Middle East conflict.

title: 'Beti, beti hoti hai': UP CM Yogi comes to defence of Akhilesh's daughter
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath strongly condemned derogatory social media comments targeting Akhilesh Yadav's daughter, deeming such remarks against any daughter unacceptable. He directed police action and urged the Samajwadi Party chief to ensure his workers also exercise restraint in their language, emphasizing the need for civility in public discourse.

title: 3-year-old cancer patient dies at AIIMS Bhopal after nurse injects formalin
content: An FIR has been registered against two nursing officers at AIIMS Bhopal following the death of a three-year-old child. An internal inquiry found gross negligence in handling formalin, a hazardous chemical, which was allegedly administered to the child through an IV line, leading to his death during treatment.

title: Nora Fatehi opens up about sharing FIFA World Cup 2026 lineup with Shakira, Burna Boy
content: Actor-dancer Nora Fatehi has been winning hearts globally after her electrifying performance at the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony. For Nora, performing at one of the world's biggest sporting events alongside internationally renowned artists like Shakira and Burna Boy marked a major milestone in her career.

title: Shubman Gill leads from the front as India's 2027 ODI WC roadmap begins
content: Shubman Gill showcased his mastery in ODI cricket with a composed unbeaten 84, guiding India to a seven-wicket victory against Afghanistan. His calm anchoring of the chase, despite a rain-truncated match and a challenging target, highlighted his leadership and focus on stabilizing the team ahead of the next ODI World Cup.

title: 'Body preserved with cold water': Indian sailor dies on ship near Oman; tensions delay aid
content: The Forward Seamen's Union of India is seeking assistance after a 35-year-old officer died on a vessel off Oman's coast. His body has been onboard for over two days without proper refrigeration, leading to a health risk for the crew. The union alleges delayed medical help due to regional tensions and is demanding body preservation, repatriation, and an investigation.

title: Haryana man behind ‘Rs 370 biryani’ joke tells ex-manager he did it for ‘entertainment’
content: A 22-year-old web developer from Haryana is facing severe consequences, including job loss and legal notices, after his remarks at a stand-up comedy show sparked widespread online backlash. Himanshu Jangra expressed deep regret and tension over the incident, claiming his comments were improvised and intended for entertainment, not to reflect his true beliefs or misogynistic intent.

title: Williamson replaced by India whitewash hero in New Zealand squad for England Test
content: Young is expected to arrive in England on Sunday and will join the squad for the remaining two Tests, the team announced on Saturday.

title: Indian-origin man stabbed to death in west London; probe under way
content: An Indian-origin man, Gurbhej Singh, was fatally stabbed in London's Southall on North Road. Police are investigating the homicide, which occurred around 00:30hrs on Wednesday. Detectives have arrested seven men, with one currently on bail pending further inquiries, as they continue to seek information.

title: Folarin Balogun and the joy of the global football
content: Folarin Balogun, scorer of two goals on his World Cup debut, was born in Brooklyn to Nigerian parents after his pregnant mother, visiting her sister in America, was reportedly advised against flying back to Britain, where she lived at that time. In the more extreme corners of the MAGA universe, that would make Balogun an "anchor baby" – born to someone visiting the US. On Thursday night, he was simply called "Man of the Match" the undisputed hero of a squad that perfectly represents the beautiful, messy, borderless reality of modern soccer.

title: 'INDIA bloc has already won 2029 elections': Rahul Gandhi lays out strategy of 'resistance with unity'
content: Rahul Gandhi boldly proclaimed that the INDIA alliance is already the victor of the 2029 Lok Sabha elections. He stressed the vital importance of uniting the opposition to challenge the BJP's perceived encroachment on democratic institutions and the silencing of dissenting voices.

title: Why did the SC quantify labour of homemakers? | Explained
content: What was the dispute that led to this ruling? What did the Court say about homemakers? What does the ruling signify?

title: In Zojila, light at the end of tunnel
content: Once a pass known for deaths due to avalanches, shooting stones, and snowfall, the newly constructed Zojila Tunnel builds on India’s growing road infrastructure and fortifies the country’s strategic interests in the region with its hostile neighbours, China and Pakistan. Peerzada Ashiq reports on how it also offers hope to locals and their economy

title: IIM-Calcutta allows deferred placements to students who are keen on entrepreneurship
content: If they decide to seek employment opportunities later on, they will be eligible to participate in the institute’s placement process the following year

title: Kaziranga releases first scientific report on greater hog badger
content: The study through camera traps was conducted by Kaziranga’s Tiger Cell in collaboration with Wildlife Conservation Trust and The Fishing Cat Project

title: Male nurse’s suicide in Jaipur sparks protests; wife to get contractual job
content: The State government has decided to remove about 6,500 contractual nursing personnel employed through private placement agencies in the government hospitals

title: Will not allow illegal oil shipments from Iran, U.S. tells India
content: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio’s warning came during discussions with Minister Jaishankar; three Indian sailors were killed recently in U.S. attacks; Rubio says ships should follow U.S. instructions

title: P&T apartment residents to hit protest path again
content: Residents, organised under the P&T Apartment Owners Association, are preparing to launch yet another protest at the GCDA headquarters in Kadavanthra on June 15, demanding an immediate solution to their woes

title: Kochi City police step up probe into alleged human trafficking case
content: Police say they have identified a few people behind the racket, besides the accused arrested on Saturday in connection with the alleged confinement of seven people in a building at Cheranalloor

title: U.P. eyes ensuring zero discharge into rivers, implements multi-layered approach including drone survey
content: “Based on the drone survey findings, a comprehensive action plan is being prepared to ensure zero discharge into rivers”

title: Gruha Lakshmi and Gruha Jyothi beneficiaries have to reapply, says Karnataka Chief Minister
content: In a move claimed to be aimed at plugging leakage, Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar has announced that beneficiaries of the Gruha Lakshmi and the Gruha Jyothi schemes, two of the five guarantee schemes in the State, need to reapply

title: Teaching fellows of constituent colleges of Anna University demand reinstatement of services
content: The teaching fellows have been employed as temporary faculty in the constituent colleges for about 10 to 15 years drawing salaries between ₹25,000 and ₹30,000

title: Pariyathukavu land dispute resolved; Dalit families to get land within disputed property
content: Govt. to build houses in the allotted plots using funds raised through sponsorship; until the houses are built, the families will be allowed to stay in their present houses

title: PM’s Europe Visit Begins in Nice, with tech and bilateral ties in focus
content: In addition to Nice, this France visit includes programmes in Evian and Paris, with a possible bilateral meeting with U.S. President Trump

title: Fruit pulp factory, civic amenities, new industries are priorities for Vellore: Minister
content: Along with traffic congestion, civic amenities like bitumen roads, regular water supply, modern solid waste management and recreational spots will be given priority, says Minister Vijay Balaji

title: Hyosung TNS eyes ATM manufacturing plant in Tamil Nadu: Industries Minister
content: Minister S. Keerthana said in a post on X that she had a productive meeting with the leadership of Hyosung TNS in South Korea, discussing opportunities in Tamil Nadu and exploring potential collaborations in advanced manufacturing and technology sectors

title: Fact-finding team will assess ground realities to acquire land for SIPCOT expansion project: Minister
content: He highlighted the need for coexistence of industrial development and agricultural activities and said that necessary land will be acquired for the project without affecting the livelihood of farmers

title: 61-day fishing ban to end in Rameswaram
content: Though the fishing ban period will end at midnight on Sunday, a decision on issuing tokens is yet to be taken due to the weather forecast of strong winds in place till June 15

title: Palaniswami says more effective use of social media yielded results for TVK in T.N. Assembly elections
content: AIADMK begins district-wise consultations with functionaries and senior leaders to review its performance in the Assembly elections

title: Why is the Zojila tunnel a game changer? | Explains
content: Why is the tunnel an engineering marvel and what were the challenges in building it? How does it improve connectivity and ensure travellers’ safety? How does it provide strategic access in the border region? When will it open for civilian traffic?

title: M.A. Baby accuses Rahul Gandhi of double standards
content: CPI(M) leader accuses Congress leader of aiding Central agencies; calls for political decorum within INDIA bloc

title: Vijayawada airport records 1,370% revenue growth in 12 years, says Director
content: The annual passenger traffic expanded by 504.12%, surging from 2,33,617 travellers in 2014-15 to 14,11,325 in 2025-26, says Airport Director Lakshmi Kantha Reddy

title: Mamata’s most trusted aide Sudip Bandyopadhyay may join camp of rebel Trinamool MPs
content: Sudip Bandyopadhyay’s visit to Delhi and meeting with Union Minister Bhupendra Yadav comes days before rebel Trinamool MPs are going to meet the Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla and stake claim as an independent group backing the Bharatiya Janata Party led National Democratic Alliance government at the Centre

title: Samiti calls for annual observance of Article 371(J) Day across seven districts of Kalyana Karnataka
content: A meeting convened by the Kalyana Karnataka Horata Samiti resolved to observe an annual Article 371(J) Day across the seven districts of Kalyana Karnataka and to organise a series of awareness programmes on the constitutional provision

title: 156 MBBS students conferred degrees at Gulbarga Institute of Medical Sciences graduation ceremony
content: The Gulbarga Institute of Medical Sciences, Kalaburagi, conducted its sixth graduation ceremony on Saturday, conferring MBBS degrees upon 156 students from the batch of 2020

title: Two nurses at AIIMS Bhopal booked for giving wrong injection to child cancer patient that caused his death
content: The nurses have been booked under BNS Sections 106 and 286 at the Bagsewania police station. While Ms. Sharma allegedly administered the wrong injection, Ms. Gujarati had left the toxic injection unattended

title: Shigella | Virulent bacterium
content: Kerala has seen a new outbreak of shigellosis, caused by this gram-negative, contagious bacterium, which infects millions globally every year

title: Centre to provide ₹10 crore rice aid for refugees in Mizoram
content: The assurance came after Chief Minister Lalduhoma highlighted the humanitarian challenges faced by the State in accommodating refugees

title: Monsoon tracker highlights: IMD forecasts thunderstorm in 11 districts of Telangana
content: According to the IMD’s latest bulletin, thunderstorms accompanied with lightning and gusty winds are very likely to occur at isolated places in 11 districts of Telangana

title: Delhi Court Refuses Interim Relief to Indian Polo Association in Jaipur Polo Ground Dispute
content: "A similar kind of request was moved before the Learned Principal District and Sessions Judge, PHC and Delhi High Court, and no relief was granted to the appellant. Therefore, keeping in view judicial discipline and propriety, I am not inclined to stay the execution of the impugned order even till next date"

title: Attacks on vessels with Indian seafarers, Iranian ‘service fee’ may feature in IORA meet
content: India is the current Chair of the Indian Ocean Rim Association; Although the IORA’s charter explicitly bars members from raising issues that do not concern regional cooperation, in the broader scheme of things, India could look to utilise the forum for airing its concerns in the maritime space

title: Watch: The ₹370 Biryani Row: What really happened
content: A stand-up comedy audience interaction has snowballed into one of India’s most talked-about controversies. Comedian Pranit More is facing legal scrutiny after viral clips from his Gurugram show triggered outrage online and prompted action from authorities.

title: Union Urban Affairs Ministry takes physical possession of Jaipur Polo Ground in the Race Course Area
content: On May 20, an eviction order was issued which sought possession of the land parcel, citing its need for a “larger public purpose”

title: Civic Body Chief's Son Arrested On Rape Charges In Bengal's Naihati
content: Tension prevailed outside the court premises as an agitated crowd, including supporters of BJP workers, allegedly pelted eggs and shoes at Chatterjee while he was being taken to court.

title: Shimla School Administrator Shot Dead Outside Campus Gate
content: Special police teams have been constituted to identify and arrest the accused, while an extensive search operation is underway, police said.

title: BJP's Punjab Rally Signals OBC Outreach Is Top Priority Before Elections
content: The BJP appears to be attempting a structural shift in Punjab by building a broader coalition among non-traditional voter groups, particularly OBC communities

title: Trump May Discuss Hormuz Demining Efforts At G7 Amid Signs Of Peace Deal
content: Britain and France, who are both members of the G7, have expressed interest in assisting with demining the critical waterway once the conflict is paused.

title: Trinamool Crisis Spirals: Ex-Minister Quits Party, MP Meets Amit Shah
content: The developments have triggered fresh political speculation amid ongoing turmoil within the Trinamool Congress.

title: Trump's Name Removed From Kennedy Centre After Court Says It's Unlawful
content: The effort came after a judge rejected a last-minute bid by the centre's board to halt the removal of Trump's name, marking a setback for the president's broader push

title: BJP Won't Be Junior Partner: Hardeep Puri On Alliance Speculation In Punjab
content: Hardeep Puri's remarks came days after Shiromani Akali Dal chief Sukhbir Singh Badal brushed aside speculation about the possible alliance with the BJP, saying that his focus was on strengthening his party.

title: Delhi Cop Rammed By Speeding Scooter, Then Run Over By Mini Truck. Dies
content: The individuals on the scooter fled the scene after the incident, while the driver of the mini-truck was caught on the spot.

title: On Camera, 1 Killed And 3 Injured As Car Rams 2 Bikes In Bengaluru
content: According to police, the incident occurred around 6:45 am on Saturday morning as the Eeco car rammed the two motorcycles.

title: 'Coming To You': Man Dies By Suicide, 2 Friends Kill Themselves Soon After
content: Two deaths within days shocked their relatives and neighbours. However, another piece of bad news awaited them.

title: Delhi Woman Rescues Sisters From Fire, Goes Back To Save Pet Cat. Now In ICU
content: Mumtaz's family said that after regaining consciousness, she repeatedly asked about their pet cat, Coco

title: Wikipedia Can Be Biased On "Disputed" Issues, Says Co-Founder Larry Sanger
content: Responding to questions about whether Wikipedia can be trusted on issues concerning India and Hindus, Sanger said the encyclopedia generally performs well when presenting straightforward and undisputed facts.

title: Tamil Nadu Woman Accuses Relatives Of Sexual Abuse Over Property Dispute
content: The survivor, an 18-year-old student, alleged that around 10 people attacked her and her family members during a confrontation in their village on June 3.

title: "Pakistan Will Disintegrate On Its Own": Himachal Governor On PoK Protest
content: Himachal Pradesh Governor Kavinder Gupta drew parallels between the current crackdown on protestors in PoJK and Pakistan's 1971 atrocities in East Pakistan that led to the creation of Bangladesh.

title: Centre Takes Possession Of Jaipur Polo Ground In Delhi
content: The Indian Polo Association termed the eviction "wrongful, arbitrary and contrary to law", and said it would pursue all available legal remedies to safeguard its rights and interests.

title: Gurugram Couple Claims IVF Clinic Swapped Samples, Twins Not Their Children
content: The test allegedly indicated that the embryo of another couple was implanted into Meenu Rathore's womb.

content: veep JD Vance will be flying out to Geneva to do the honors . the deal is scheduled to get signed tomorrow, and immediately after it is signed, the Hormuz Strait is OPEN TO ALL . he did not reveal much else about the terms of the deal .

title: Pakistan-occupied Kashmir resident apprehended near LoC
content:
Representative image

SRINAGAR: A 19-year-old man from Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) was apprehended in Karnah sector of Kashmir’s Kupwara district on Friday soon after he crossed the heavily guarded LoC. “Acting on specific intelligence inputs, a joint team of Army and J&K police intercepted the individual near Simri village in Kupwara and prevented any potential security breach,” Army said. Police said the intruder, Assad Khan, from Muzaffarabad, was being questioned to ascertain the circumstances under which he entered India. Khan is the second person from PoK to have been detained near LoC in Kashmir in the past two weeks. On June 1, one Zeeshan Ahmad (22) was detained for crossing LoC into Uri sector of Baramulla district.

End of Article

title: Rubio’s words were of command, not contrition: Congress
content: congress slams american secretary of state Marco Rubio's statement on the strike . he reportedly declared that failure to comply with orders of the US military will not be tolerated . the statement contains absolutely no expression of regret or condolence for the loss of innocent Indian lives .

title: Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth appointed next Army chief, to take charge on June 30
content: the govt has appointed Lieutenant general Dhiraj Seth as the next chief army with effect from June 30 . he has had extensive experience across the operational, strategic, capability development and institutional domains . his command assignments include an armoured Regiment in the desert sector, an Armoured Brigade in the western theatre, and a counter-insurgency force in J&K .
